question: Do white blood cells outnumber red blood cells in the human body?
context: Red blood cells are about 40-50% of what makes up human blood.
White blood cells make up about 1% of the blood in a human body.
response:
<reasoning>
Red blood cells make up 40-50% of human blood.
White blood cells make up about 1% of human blood.
40-50% is greater than 1%.
White blood cells do not outnumber red blood cells in the human body.
<answer> No
claims: Red blood cells make up 40-50% of human blood
White blood cells make up about 1% of human blood
White blood cells do not outnumber red blood cells in the human body
